summary: In this extremely brief post, Joel Spolsky makes a self-deprecating observation about software developers' lack of self-awareness regarding their own knowledge gaps. He sets up the joke by asking what drives him crazy, acknowledges his reputation for being easily irritated, then delivers the punchline: most developers don't realize how little they know. He closes by pointing to himself as an example, linking to a book review.
key_points: Most software developers lack awareness of how much they don't know about their own field
Self-awareness about knowledge gaps is rare and valuable in software development
Joel includes himself among those with limited knowledge, modeling intellectual humility
money_quotes: What drives me crazy is that most software developers don't realize just how little they know about software development.
key_insight: The biggest problem in software development isn't ignorance itself, but developers' lack of awareness of their own ignorance.
